benchmark.subtitle: Track rendering and computational pressure before runtime regressions.

welcome.title: Animator workspace ready
welcome.description: Load a rig and inspect rendering, meshes, clipping, blend, and physics in one pass.

warnings.spineVersion: Warning: This file uses Spine 4.1. The benchmark is designed for Spine 4.2. Version will be adjusted automatically.

appErrorBoundary.title: Something went wrong
appErrorBoundary.description: The app hit an unexpected error. Reload to continue.

recommendations.reduceBoneDepth: Reduce bone hierarchy depth by flattening the structure where possible.
recommendations.reduceTotalBones: Consider reducing the total number of bones by simplifying the skeleton.
recommendations.reduceVertices: Reduce the total number of vertices across all meshes.
recommendations.minimizeDeformedMeshes: Minimize the number of deformed meshes, especially those with high vertex counts.
recommendations.reduceWeightedMeshes: Reduce the number of meshes with bone weights, as they require more calculations.
recommendations.limitClippingMasks: Limit the number of clipping masks as they significantly impact performance.
recommendations.simplifyComplexMasks: Simplify complex clipping masks to use fewer vertices (4 or less is optimal).
recommendations.reduceNonNormalBlendModes: Reduce the number of non-normal blend modes to minimize render state changes.
recommendations.minimizeAdditiveBlendModes: Minimize the use of additive blend modes as they are particularly expensive.
recommendations.reducePhysicsConstraints: Physics constraints are particularly expensive - consider reducing their number or complexity.
recommendations.simplifyIkConstraints: Simplify IK constraints by reducing chain length or number of affected bones.
recommendations.optimizePathConstraints: Optimize path constraints by simplifying paths or reducing the number of constrained bones.
recommendations.physicsUsage: Physics constraints are used in {{used}} out of {{total}} animations. Consider baking physics simulation for static animations.
recommendations.clippingFound: Clipping masks found in: {{names}}{{more}}. Consider using alpha blending or pre-masked textures instead.
recommendations.highVertexAnimations: {{count}} animations have high vertex counts (>500). Consider simplifying meshes for: {{names}}
recommendations.focusPoorAnimations: Focus optimization on these poor-performing animations: {{names}}
recommendations.multiIssueAnimations: {{count}} animations combine multiple expensive features. Consider simplifying: {{names}}
recommendations.performanceGenerallyGood: Performance is generally good across all animations. Minor optimizations may still be possible in specific areas.

excellent.description: Suitable for all platforms and continuous animations

good.description: Works well on most platforms but may have issues on low-end devices

moderate.description: May cause performance dips, especially with multiple instances

poor.description: Performance issues likely on most devices

interpretations.excellent: Suitable for all platforms and continuous animations
interpretations.good: Works well on most platforms but may have issues on low-end devices
interpretations.moderate: May cause performance dips, especially with multiple instances
interpretations.poor: Performance issues likely on most devices
interpretations.veryPoor: Significant performance issues on all devices

mesh.title: Mesh Statistics

notes.title: Mesh Performance Impact:
notes.vertexCount: Vertex Count: Each vertex requires memory and processing time. High vertex counts (>50) have significant impact.
notes.deformation: Deformation: Deforming meshes requires extra calculations per frame - {{factor}}× more costly than static meshes.
notes.boneWeights: Bone Weights: Each bone weight adds matrix multiplication operations - {{factor}}× more impact per weighted vertex.
notes.optimizationTip: Optimization Tip: Use fewer vertices for meshes that deform or have bone weights. Consider using Region attachments for simple shapes.

mesh.topMeshesNote: Showing top 10 meshes by vertex count. Total: {{count}} meshes

clipping.title: Clipping Masks
clipping.noMasks: No clipping masks found in this skeleton.

notes.title: Notes on Clipping Masks:
notes.highImpact: High Impact: Clipping masks are one of the most expensive operations in Spine rendering
notes.vertexCount: Vertex Count: Each vertex in a mask increases the computational cost
notes.optimalConfiguration: Optimal Configuration: Use triangular or quadrilateral masks (3-4 vertices) whenever possible
notes.gpuCost: GPU Cost: Each clipping mask requires additional GPU rendering passes (stencil buffer operations)
notes.recommendation: Recommendation: Limit to 2-3 masks per skeleton, with fewer than 6 vertices each

notes.title: Notes on Blend Modes:
notes.normalBlendMode: Normal Blend Mode: Most efficient, requires a single rendering pass
notes.nonNormalBlendModes: Non-Normal Blend Modes: Each requires a separate render pass or shader switch
notes.renderingCost: Rendering Cost: Each blend mode change forces a renderer "flush" operation
notes.additiveBlend: Additive Blend: Higher cost than normal blend due to blending calculations
notes.multiplyBlend: Multiply Blend: Similar to additive, requires additional GPU operations
notes.recommendation: Recommendation: Limit to 2 non-normal blend modes per skeleton
notes.frameByFrameDescription: Values shown are the maximum number of blend modes visible at any single frame.
notes.impactDescription: Non-normal blend modes require additional GPU render passes.
notes.concurrentDescription: Having 10 blend modes where only 2 are visible at once is much better than having 10 visible simultaneously.
notes.optimizationDescription: Use normal blend mode when possible, pre-composite effects for static elements.

notes.title: Notes on Bone Structure:
notes.boneCount: Bone Count: Each bone requires matrix computations every frame
notes.hierarchyDepth: Hierarchy Depth: Deep hierarchies increase transformation complexity exponentially
notes.recommendation: Recommendation: Keep bone hierarchies under 5 levels deep when possible
notes.optimalStructure: Optimal Structure: Flat hierarchies with few parent-child relationships perform better

physics.title: Constraints Analysis

formula.title: Performance Impact Formula:
formula.explanation: Where constraintImpact is a weighted sum of IK, transform, path, and physics constraint impacts

physics.noConstraints: No constraints found in this skeleton.

notes.title: Notes on Constraints:
notes.ikConstraints: IK Constraints: Cost increases with bone chain length and iteration count
notes.physicsConstraints: Physics Constraints: Highest performance impact, especially with multiple affected properties
notes.pathConstraints: Path Constraints: Complex path curves and ChainScale rotate mode are more expensive
notes.transformConstraints: Transform Constraints: Each affected property (position, rotation, scale) adds calculation overhead
notes.recommendation: Recommendation: Use constraints sparingly and with minimal bone chains when possible

vertices.label: Vertices
vertices.highNote: High vertex density for this mesh.

weights.label: Weights
weights.weightedNote: Weighted mesh can increase update cost.

filterDeformed.label: Fix now: filter deformed meshes
filterDeformed.impact: Expected impact: isolate expensive deformation tracks.

isolateMesh.label: Fix now: isolate selected mesh
isolateMesh.impact: Expected impact: immediate canvas confirmation of highlighted mesh.

sendAtlasHint.label: Fix now: send atlas grouping hint
sendAtlasHint.impact: Expected impact: reduced page transitions near this mesh.

explainer.what: This mesh uses {{vertices}} vertices and {{triangles}} triangles on atlas page "{{atlasPage}}".
explainer.whyNowHigh: Vertex count is high enough to create avoidable cost spikes on dense animation frames.
explainer.whyNowHeadroom: Optimization headroom exists and can help stabilize worst-case playback.
explainer.howToFix: Reduce overclustered regions, trim hidden deformation keys, and simplify weighted areas where fidelity is not visible.
explainer.howToVerify: Run optimization preview, compare proof cards, and confirm visual quality in the highlighted mesh overlay.

drawCalls.label: Draw Calls
drawCalls.note: Total calls in current draw order

filterPage.label: Fix now: filter page {{page}}
filterPage.impact: Expected impact: isolate contiguous runs to reveal merge opportunities.

isolate.label: Fix now: isolate selected draw call
isolate.impact: Expected impact: immediate visual verification on canvas.

atlasSuggestion.label: Fix now: atlas grouping suggestion
atlasSuggestion.impact: Expected impact: reduce page transitions with grouped pack targets.

explainer.what: This row draws "{{attachment}}" from "{{atlasPage}}" with "{{blendMode}}" blend.
explainer.whyNowBreak: It currently causes a batch break, so the frame is paying an extra draw call.
explainer.whyNowStable: It is part of a stable batch, so changes here should preserve existing ordering and blend behavior.
explainer.howToFix: Group adjacent regions onto the same atlas page and normalize blend mode where artist intent allows. Keep draw order blocks contiguous for shared materials.
explainer.howToVerify: Re-run this route and confirm lower page/blend break counts. Keep the selected row pinned and compare before/after proof cards.

insight.title: {{regionName}}

page.label: Atlas Page
page.note: Region occupies approximately {{pageShare}}% of this page footprint.

breakRisk.label: Break Risk
breakRisk.problematicNote: Current region is involved in draw call breaks.
breakRisk.stableNote: Current region is currently batching safely.

regionSize.label: Region Size
regionSize.note: Use this to evaluate whether grouping opportunities are realistic.

filterProblematic.label: Fix now: filter problematic regions
filterProblematic.impact: Expected impact: isolate break-causing regions first.

isolatePage.label: Fix now: isolate page {{pageName}}
isolatePage.impact: Expected impact: tighter packing decisions with less visual noise.

jumpDrawCalls.label: Fix now: jump to Draw Calls
jumpDrawCalls.impact: Expected impact: validate page break reduction on slot order.

explainer.what: This region "{{regionName}}" is packed on atlas page "{{pageName}}".
explainer.whyNowProblematic: It aligns with current draw-call break points and is a prime target for immediate batching wins.
explainer.whyNowStable: It is currently stable but still useful for validating future packing changes.
explainer.howToFix: Group regions that render sequentially onto the same page, prioritize high-frequency slots, and repack to reduce cross-page transitions.
explainer.howToVerify: Return to Draw Calls route and confirm lower page-break count while the retained selection stays focused on this region.
